title: linaria concolor f. serpens
intro: Linaria concolor f. serpens, also known as Toadflax or Butter and Eggs, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Linaria concolor f. serpens is often dried and made into tea or infusions, or used in tinctures and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
